response.justification: Bogdan brings genuine Staff-level credentials, current hands-on experience building AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling, and a proven distributed-systems track record (millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby). However, Python — listed as a must-have — is entirely absent from both the skills section and employment history, which is a significant gap. The AI-native engineering experience, while directly relevant, is only about two months old (re:cinq, April 2025). The candidate's breadth across languages and systems is an asset, and the current role context aligns well with the job's agentic-systems baseline, making a screening conversation worthwhile to probe Python depth and operational ownership.
strengths: Current role at re:cinq directly involves AI-agent infrastructure and MCP/agentic-workflow tooling — matches the AI-native baseline expectation of the role
Proven high-throughput distributed-systems experience at Dunnhumby: micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests/second using async I/O and low-latency design
Consistent Staff-level scope across multiple employers with evidence of technical breadth, community leadership (workshops, talks), and open-source ownership
concerns: Python is completely absent from the skills list and all employment history — this is a stated must-have and represents a concrete disqualifier unless depth exists undisclosed
Hands-on AI/agentic experience is limited to roughly two months at the current employer; no prior LLM or ML product background is evident
No explicit evidence of cross-functional stakeholder management, incident-response ownership, or on-call operational experience — all required non-technical skills
